questions.question: When is the voter registration deadline?
questions.answer: The deadline varies by state, typically 15-30 days before Election Day. Some states offer same-day registration. Check your state's specific deadline using our timeline tool.

questions.question: Can I register to vote online?
questions.answer: Most states offer online voter registration. You'll typically need a driver's license or state ID. Check if your state offers online registration in our state guide.

questions.question: What if I miss the registration deadline?
questions.answer: Some states offer same-day registration at polling places or early voting sites. If your state doesn't, you'll need to wait until the next election. Check your state's rules.

questions.question: What is early voting?
questions.answer: Early voting allows you to cast your ballot in person before Election Day. Not all states offer early voting. Check your state's early voting period and locations.

questions.question: How do I vote by mail?
questions.answer: Request an absentee/mail ballot from your election office. Some states send ballots automatically. Complete and return your ballot by the deadline - typically it must be postmarked by Election Day.

questions.question: Do I need an ID to vote?
questions.answer: ID requirements vary by state. Some require photo ID, others accept various forms of identification, and some require no ID at all. Check your state's specific requirements.

questions.question: What should I bring to the polling place?
questions.answer: Bring your ID if required by your state. It's also helpful to bring your voter registration card and a sample ballot if you've prepared one. Check your state's ID requirements.

questions.question: What if my name isn't on the voter list?
questions.answer: Ask to vote with a provisional ballot. Election officials will verify your registration afterward. You may also be able to register on the spot if your state allows same-day registration.

questions.question: What are polling hours?
questions.answer: Most polling places are open from early morning until evening, but hours vary by state. Typical hours are 6-7 AM to 7-8 PM. If you're in line when polls close, you can still vote.
